translationBanner.text: This translation is a draft awaiting review by a native speaker.

trust.privacy: AidPilot stores nothing about you. Everything you enter stays on your device. We never submit anything for you and never see your name, SSN, or finances.
trust.cadaa: Information you provide on the California Dream Act Application is used only to determine eligibility for state financial aid. It is never shared with the federal government or used for immigration enforcement.
trust.cadaaTitle: About the CA Dream Act Application

landing.title: From “haven’t started” to a finished aid worksheet.
landing.subtitle: AidPilot walks you and your family through the FAFSA or the CA Dream Act Application — in English, Spanish, or Vietnamese. No account, and nothing about you is stored.
landing.stepsTitle: Three steps to a finished worksheet.
landing.step1Body: Answer three quick questions to see which application fits your situation — FAFSA, CADAA, or a visit to your counselor.
landing.step2Body: Preview every section ahead of time and check off what you’ve reviewed, in your language.
landing.step3Body: Download a preparation worksheet, then sign and submit on the official site yourself.
landing.disclaimer: AidPilot is an independent guide. It is not affiliated with the U.S. Department of Education or the California Student Aid Commission, and it never submits anything for you. Verify details with your counselor — you sign and submit on the official application site yourself.

triage._verify: VERIFY against CSAC 'which application is right for me' before launch.
triage.title: Let’s find the right application.
triage.subtitle: Three quick questions. Nothing you answer is saved or sent anywhere.
triage.summaryTitle: Based on your answers, your path is:

citizenship.label: Are you a U.S. citizen or eligible noncitizen (e.g., permanent resident / green card holder)?

parentSsn.label: Will the parent(s) on your application have a Social Security Number?

senior.label: Are you a current California high school senior or a recent graduate heading to college this year?

counselor.notSeniorNote: TODO: Placeholder note — this guide is designed for current California seniors and recent graduates. (Human-sourced content pending.)

walkthrough.title: Go through the form, section by section.
walkthrough.subtitle: Check off each item as you review it. Your progress stays on this device — nothing is saved or sent.

noPath.title: Let’s find your path first.
noPath.body: Answer three quick questions so we can show the right sections for you.

contributorFsaId.title: Contributors & the FSA ID
contributorFsaId.body:
A contributor is anyone required to provide information on the FAFSA: you (the student), your spouse, a biological or adoptive parent, or a parent’s spouse (stepparent). Grandparents, foster parents, legal guardians, siblings, and aunts or uncles are NOT contributors. Being a contributor does not make someone responsible for paying for college.

Every student and contributor needs their own StudentAid.gov account (FSA ID). It is a legally binding signature — never share it. For 2026–27, verification is instant for SSN holders (no more 1–3 day wait). You’ll need: your SSN (if you have one), full legal name, date of birth, your own email, and your own mobile number.

A parent or spouse without an SSN CAN make an account and complete their section — they verify with an ITIN, mailing address, and ID documents, which can take longer.

You and every contributor must give consent to import IRS tax information — even if they didn’t file a tax return. If anyone refuses consent, no federal aid.

What you’ll need: your Social Security number if you have one, your 2024 federal tax return and W-2s (the 2026–27 form uses 2024 tax information), records of untaxed income such as child support received, and current balances of checking, savings, and any investments — plus business or farm records if that applies.

Create your StudentAid.gov account before you start.

File as early as you can — the form opens around October 1. Much state aid is first-come, first-served, and California’s priority deadline is March 2.

collegeList.label: Colleges to receive your information
collegeList.whatItMeans: You can list up to 20 colleges. Add any school you might apply to, even if you’re unsure — you can remove one later.
collegeList.commonError: Adding a school late can cost you aid — add every school you might apply to now; you can remove one later.

irsDataConsent.label: Consent to transfer federal tax information
irsDataConsent.whatItMeans: You and each contributor must give consent and approval to import federal tax information from the IRS — required for federal aid even if you didn’t file taxes. Once you consent, your 2024 tax data imports automatically, and you won’t be able to see or edit the imported numbers (a security feature).
irsDataConsent.commonError: If anyone refuses consent, no federal aid.

taxFilingStatus.label: Tax filing status
taxFilingStatus.whatItMeans: If you didn’t file a 2024 tax return, use your 2024 income information and enter 0 where questions don’t apply.

studentIncome.label: Student income
studentIncome.whatItMeans: The 2026–27 form uses your 2024 tax information.
studentIncome.documentNeeded: Your 2024 federal tax return and W-2s, plus records of untaxed income such as child support received.

studentAssets.label: Student assets
studentAssets.whatItMeans: Report the current balances of your cash, savings, and investments as of the day you sign — not the tax-year amounts.
studentAssets.documentNeeded: Current balances of checking, savings, and any investments — plus business or farm records if that applies.
studentAssets.commonError: Don’t include your parents’ assets in your section — report your own assets; your parent reports theirs separately.

whichParent.label: Which parent(s) must contribute
whichParent.whatItMeans: If your parents are married or living together, both parents’ information goes on the FAFSA — both are contributors unless they filed taxes jointly (then one reports for both). If they are divorced or separated and NOT living together, only the parent who gave you more financial support in the last 12 months files; if support was equal, the one with higher income and assets. This replaced the old “who you live with” rule.
whichParent.documentNeeded: None — use the free “Who’s My FAFSA Parent?” wizard on StudentAid.gov (no account needed).
whichParent.commonError: If the filing parent remarried, the stepparent’s information is required — prenuptial agreements are ignored.

parentSsnStatus.label: Parent Social Security Number
parentSsnStatus.whatItMeans: A parent or spouse without an SSN CAN make a StudentAid.gov account and complete their section. They verify with an ITIN, mailing address, and ID documents, which can take longer. They can enter income without a tax return and are not asked about citizenship status.
parentSsnStatus.documentNeeded: ITIN (if they have one), mailing address, and ID documents.
parentSsnStatus.commonError: On the invite, check “my parent doesn’t have an SSN” — it then asks for the parent’s mailing address, which must match the address on their FSA ID or the accounts won’t link.

parentIrsDataConsent.label: Parent consent to transfer federal tax information
parentIrsDataConsent.whatItMeans: Every contributor must give consent and approval to import federal tax information from the IRS — even if they didn’t file a tax return. Once they consent, their 2024 tax data imports automatically and can’t be seen or edited (a security feature).
parentIrsDataConsent.commonError: If any contributor refuses consent, no federal aid.

parentTaxFilingStatus.label: Parent tax filing status
parentTaxFilingStatus.whatItMeans: If they didn’t file a 2024 tax return, use their 2024 income information and enter 0 where questions don’t apply.

parentIncome.label: Parent income
parentIncome.commonError: If income dropped since 2024 (a job loss, for example), complete the form as instructed, then contact the school’s financial aid office to request an aid adjustment — you can’t update a processed FAFSA with new tax information yourself.

parentAssets.label: Parent assets
parentAssets.whatItMeans: Report the current balances of cash, savings, and investments as of the day you sign — not the tax-year amounts. Parents report their assets separately from the student’s.

contributorAccount.title: Your CSAC account & contributors
contributorAccount.body:
The California Dream Act Application (CADAA) is California’s state aid application. Undocumented students file it INSTEAD of the FAFSA to get state and institutional aid — Cal Grants, community college fee waivers, university grants, private scholarships — and in-state tuition. It does NOT provide federal aid.

DACA students file the CADAA, not the FAFSA.

U.S. citizens and green-card holders with undocumented parent(s) or spouse may file the CADAA instead of the FAFSA if they don’t want to submit a FAFSA — doing so forgoes federal aid.

Create your login at dream.csac.ca.gov before you start.

What you’ll need: an ITIN if you or your parents file taxes with one (no SSN? leave it blank or enter all zeroes), your 2024 tax information and W-2s (the 2026–27 form uses 2024 tax information), records of untaxed income such as child support received, and current balances of checking, savings, and any investments — plus business or farm records if that applies.

File as early as you can — the form opens around October 1. Much state aid is first-come, first-served, and California’s priority deadline is March 2.

ssnOrItin.label: SSN or ITIN (if you have one)
ssnOrItin.whatItMeans: Entering an SSN or ITIN is optional. Enter your ITIN if you have one; if you have neither, leave it blank or enter all zeroes.

citizenshipStatus.label: Citizenship status
citizenshipStatus.whatItMeans: Undocumented, DACA, U-visa, and TPS students select “neither citizen nor eligible noncitizen.”
citizenshipStatus.commonError: Indicating you are a citizen or eligible noncitizen redirects you to the FAFSA.

ab540Eligibility.label: AB 540 eligibility
ab540Eligibility.whatItMeans: AB 540 generally means 3 years at California schools plus graduation. The AB 540 affidavit is embedded in the CADAA.

caCollegeList.label: California colleges to receive your information
caCollegeList.whatItMeans: You can list up to 20 colleges. Add any school you might apply to, even if you’re unsure — you can remove one later.
caCollegeList.commonError: Adding a school late can cost you aid — add every school you might apply to now; you can remove one later.

taxFilingStatus.label: Tax filing status
taxFilingStatus.whatItMeans: If you haven’t filed your 2024 taxes yet, choose “will file,” use estimates, and update after filing. If you choose “not going to file,” you still report income — W-2 box 1 plus box 8, or net profit if self-employed.
taxFilingStatus.documentNeeded: W-2s (box 1 and box 8), or records of net profit if self-employed.
taxFilingStatus.commonError: Earning over the IRS filing threshold flags the form and requires getting an ITIN and filing.

studentIncome.label: Student income
studentIncome.whatItMeans: The 2026–27 CADAA uses 2024 tax information. The CADAA does not import IRS data — you enter income and tax information manually.

parentTaxFilingStatus.label: Parent tax filing status
parentTaxFilingStatus.whatItMeans: If they haven’t filed their 2024 taxes yet, choose “will file,” use estimates, and update after filing. If “not going to file,” income is still reported — W-2 box 1 plus box 8, or net profit if self-employed.

parentIncome.label: Parent income
parentIncome.whatItMeans: Dependent students provide parent household and income information.
parentIncome.commonError: If circumstances changed, complete the form as best you can, submit, then talk to the school’s financial aid office.

studentSignature.label: Student signature
studentSignature.whatItMeans: After you submit, CSAC emails a nine-digit Dream ID. Use it to create a WebGrants 4 Students account to check your status.
studentSignature.documentNeeded: Cal Grant GPA verification — submit it by March 2.

parentSignature.label: Parent signature
parentSignature.whatItMeans: For dependent students, the parent signs with a Parent PIN.
parentSignature.commonError: Save the Parent PIN — it’s reused every year.

walkthrough._verify: VERIFY section/field structure against the current-year FAFSA (studentaid.gov) and CADAA (dream.csac.ca.gov) before launch.

worksheet.disclaimerBody: Verify with your counselor. You must sign and submit on the official government site yourself. AidPilot is not affiliated with the U.S. Department of Education or the California Student Aid Commission.

report.prompt: Did you finish your application on the official site?
report.body: Let your counselor know — anonymously. We count one more finished application, never who you are.
report.error: Couldn’t record that right now. Please try again later.
report.unconfigured: Anonymous reporting isn’t set up in this environment yet.
report.privacyNote: No name, no answers, no identifiers — just an anonymous +1.
